chapter_title.en: Places Where Prayer is Permissable and Where it is Not Permissable

text: رِوَايَةِ زُرَارَةَ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «إِذَا كَانَ بَيْنَهَا وَ بَيْنَهُ قَدْرُ مَا يُتَخَطَّى أَوْ قَدْرُ عَظْمِ ذِرَاعٍ فَصَاعِداً فَلاَ بَأْسَ إِنْ صَلَّتْ بِحِذَاهُ وَحْدَهَا.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.747 - In the narration of Zurarah from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), said:
"If there is a distance between her and him that is about the length of a step or about the length of a cubit or more, then there is no harm if She prays beside him alone."
